steps.title: Identify the goal
steps.content: We start with $P = \frac{W}{t}$ and need to isolate $W$ on one side of the equation to express it in terms of $P$ and $t$.

steps.title: Multiply both sides by t to isolate W
steps.content:
Multiply both sides by $t$:
$$P \cdot t = \frac{W}{t} \cdot t$$
The $t$ cancels on the right side:
$$W = Pt$$
This matches choice A — A is correct.
